perturbation.original_text: 1. The Contract Partner receiving the Information ( „Recipient “) will not disclose the Information received to third parties or use the Information fo r own business purposes for any other purpose except the Purpose of Exchange, without first obtai ning the written agreement of the Discloser. This applies explicitly also to the use of Informat ion for the purpose of registering patents, brands, and other intellectual property. The Recipi ent will ensure that the Information is only disclosed to those employees who need to know the s ame for the Purpose of Exchange and who explicitly know they owe a duty of confidence t o the Discloser and who are bound by obligations equivalent to those in clauses 1 and 2. The Discloser may request from the Recipient a non-disclosure obligation of the employee at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. The Contract Partner receiving the Information ( „Recipient “) will not disclose the Information received to third parties or use the Information fo r own business purposes for any other purpose except the Purpose of Exchange, without first obtai ning the written agreement of the Discloser. This restriction does not apply to the use of Information for the purpose of registering patents, brands, and other intellectual property. The Recipi ent will ensure that the Information is only disclosed to those employees who need to know the s ame for the Purpose of Exchange and who explicitly know they owe a duty of confidence t o the Discloser and who are bound by obligations equivalent to those in clauses 1 and 2. The Discloser may request from the Recipient a non-disclosure obligation of the employee at any time.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text explicitly prohibits the use of information for registering intellectual property without written agreement. The changed text removes this restriction, now allowing the Recipient to use the Information for registering patents, brands, etc., creating uncertainty about what is allowed regarding intellectual property registration. This creates a contradiction in what the Recipient can do with the information for intellectual property.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The Contract Partner receiving the Information ( „Recipient “) will not disclose the Information received to third parties or use the Information fo r own business purposes for any other purpose except the Purpose of Exchange, without first obtai ning the written agreement of the Discloser. This restriction does not apply to the use of Information for the purpose of registering patents, brands, and other intellectual property. The Recipi ent will ensure that the Information is only disclosed to those employees who need to know the s ame for the Purpose of Exchange and who explicitly know they owe a duty of confidence t o the Discloser and who are bound by obligations equivalent to those in clauses 1 and 2. The Discloser may request from the Recipient a non-disclosure obligation of the employee at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by removing the restriction on using the Information for registering patents, brands, and other intellectual property. The original text explicitly prohibited this without written agreement, while the changed text now allows it. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this case, but the introduction of the changed text, now creates a contradiction because the original text said one thing, but the Changed Text now allows the Recipient to use the Information for registering patents, brands, etc., creating uncertainty about what is allowed regarding intellectual property registration. This creates a contradiction in what the Recipient can do with the information for intellectual property.

perturbation.original_text: 6. An assignment of the rights arising from this ag reement is not permitted. There is mutual agreement, however, that the rights granted in this agreement are due to the customer of the Discloser within the framework of an agreement in f avor of a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. An assignment of the rights arising from this ag reement is permitted under special circumstances which are specified by the company. There is mutual agreement, however, that the rights granted in this agreement are due to the customer of the Discloser within the framework of an agreement in f avor of a third party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that assignment of rights is not permitted. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that assignment of rights is permitted under special circumstances. This creates an in-text contradiction as to whether assignment is allowed or not, leading to potential disagreements about the transferability of rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. An assignment of the rights arising from this ag reement is permitted under special circumstances which are specified by the company. There is mutual agreement, however, that the rights granted in this agreement are due to the customer of the Discloser within the framework of an agreement in f avor of a third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that assignment of rights is not permitted. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that assignment of rights is permitted under special circumstances. This creates an in-text contradiction within the same section, because the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same. This directly breaks compatibility and makes enforcement unclear because it states that assignment is not permitted but then that it is permitted under special circumstances, leading to potential disagreements about the transferability of rights, which adheres to the definition of inconsistencies as an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Amendments or modifications to this agreement r equire the written form. This also applies to an amendment or modification of this requirement of the written form.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Amendments or modifications to this agreement r equire the written form. Amendments or modifications to this agreement can also be done through electronic means.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that all changes to the agreement, including the requirement for written changes, must be in writing. The changed text directly contradicts this by allowing amendments and modifications to be done through electronic means. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the required form for making changes, leading to uncertainty about how modifications can be validly implemented.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Amendments or modifications to this agreement r equire the written form. Amendments or modifications to this agreement can also be done through electronic means.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear and direct contradiction. The original text requires amendments to be in writing, but the changed text allows for electronic modifications. This directly conflicts with the initial requirement, creating uncertainty about the valid methods for amending the agreement. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, confirming the direct in-text contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation states that the change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the required form for making changes.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The Contract Partners agree that there is no ade quate legal protection against any injury arising from this agreement due to the special nature of th e Information to be kept secret. Any injury to the non-disclosure obligation would lead to irrepar able damage to the Discloser. The Recipient therefore undertakes to inform the Discloser withou t delay as soon as has gained such knowledge himself of any unpermitted disclosure or unpermitted use as laid down in this agreement of any Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The Contract Partners agree that there is adequate legal protection against any injury arising from this agreement due to the special nature of th e Information to be kept secret. Any injury to the non-disclosure obligation would lead to irrepar able damage to the Discloser. The Recipient therefore undertakes to inform the Discloser withou t delay as soon as has gained such knowledge himself of any unpermitted disclosure or unpermitted use as laid down in this agreement of any Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that there is 'no adequate legal protection'. The changed text asserts the opposite, stating that there 'is adequate legal protection'. This introduces an in-text contradiction about the legal remedies available for breaches of the agreement. The ambiguity about the availability of legal protection directly impacts the risk assessment for both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The Contract Partners agree that there is adequate legal protection against any injury arising from this agreement due to the special nature of th e Information to be kept secret. Any injury to the non-disclosure obligation would lead to irrepar able damage to the Discloser. The Recipient therefore undertakes to inform the Discloser withou t delay as soon as has gained such knowledge himself of any unpermitted disclosure or unpermitted use as laid down in this agreement of any Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'no adequate legal protection', while the changed text states 'is adequate legal protection'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. Thus, the change directly contradicts the previous assertion about legal protection within the same section, satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
